_meta.purpose: Product features, user flows, route map, and current capabilities

ticket_request_flow.description: Public buyer requests tickets through event page
steps: 1. Buyer visits /e/:eventSlug
2. Fills name/email/phone + ticket type selections
3. Uploads payment evidence (required for paid events)
4. OTP sent to buyer email for verification
5. Buyer verifies OTP
6. TicketRequest created (status: PENDING_VERIFICATION)
7. Organizer reviews in dashboard → approve or reject
8. On approval: tickets generated ON-DEMAND, buyer emailed client dashboard link
9. Buyer visits /client?token=... to view QR codes
10. Buyer shows QR at door; organizer scans via dashboard scanner
ticket_request_flow.auto_approve_path: If organizer has auto-approve enabled, step 7-8 fire immediately on submission
blocking_conditions: Event at capacity (all ticket types sold out)

organizer_onboarding_flow.description: New organizer sets up their first event
steps: 1. Visit /dashboard
2. Enter organizer name → organizerAccessCode generated on first event save
3. Configure event: name, date, location, ticket types/prices
4. Verify email via OTP to publish event (emailVerified gate)
5. Event published → share /e/:slug link with buyers
6. Organizer uses dashboard to manage requests, chat, scanner
organizer_onboarding_flow.access_model: accessCode (6-char alphanumeric) is the organizer's login credential

scan_flow.description: Organizer scans QR at event door
steps: 1. Organizer opens scanner (inside dashboard OR at /scanner route)
2. Camera or manual entry of ticketPublicId or full URL
3. POST /api/scans with accessCode + ticketPublicId
4. Backend validates: ticket belongs to event, not used, not invalidated, not expired, scanner not locked
scan_flow.outcome_notes: BLOCKED fires when UserEvent.scannerLocked=true (set by admin). ALREADY_USED maps to USED in DB. INVALID_TICKET maps to INVALID in DB.
scan_flow.security: Cross-organizer tickets always return INVALID_TICKET without ticket details

chat_flow.attachments: IMAGE and PDF via multipart upload, stored locally, served via auth-checked routes

admin_flow.description: Internal admin panel for system oversight
capabilities: View all events, tickets, scans, deliveries

ticket_lifecycle.generation_model: ON-DEMAND at approval time (not pre-generated pool)

ticket_request_lifecycle.note: CANCELLED is organizer-initiated; includes cancellation message to buyer

sales_controls.salesCutoffAt: DateTime — hard cutoff after which no new requests
sales_controls.salesWindowStart: HH:MM string — daily window open time (24h)
sales_controls.salesWindowEnd: HH:MM string — daily window close time (24h)
sales_controls.maxTicketsPerEmail: Int — max APPROVED+PENDING_VERIFICATION tickets per buyer email per event

promoter_system.metrics: requests, approved tickets, scanned entries per promoter

active: PUBLIC_EVENT_PAGE (client dashboard link emailed on approval)
delivery_methods.schema_artifacts_kept: TicketDelivery model + EMAIL_LINK/PDF_DOWNLOAD enum kept for historical data

og_social_preview.mechanism: nginx routes crawler User-Agents to backend GET /e/:slug which returns static HTML with OG tags
og_social_preview.real_users: get React SPA directly (no backend hit)

missing_product_capabilities: No organizer insights dashboard (conversion rate, peak times)
No multi-event view for organizers (only single access-code context)
No monetization layer (planned: $1/scanned ticket, not yet built)
